title: There is / There are
explanation:
There is / There are

Use there is (there's) + singular and there are + plural to say something exists.

  • There's a book on the table.
  • There are two cats in the garden.

Questions: Is there…? Are there…?

questions.text: [*There is|There are|It is] a supermarket near my house.
[*There are|There is|They are] three bedrooms in the flat.
[*Is there|Are there|Does there] a bank in this street?
[*There aren't|There isn't|They aren't] any eggs in the fridge.
[*There's|There are|It has] a problem with my computer.
[*Are there|Is there|Do there] many students in your class?

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: ____ a lot of traffic today. | *There's | There are | It is
____ some apples on the table. | *There are | There is | They are
____ any milk left? | *Is there | Are there | Does
____ not many people here. | *There are | There is | They are
[*There was|There were|It was] a good film on TV last night.
[*There were|There was|They were] lots of people at the concert.
[*There isn't|There aren't|It isn't] time to finish.
[*There's|There are|It is] nothing in the box.
